skill.name: data-analysis
skill.description: Analyze datasets, clean data, calculate statistics, create visualizations, and produce structured insight reports.
skill.summary: Guides repeatable data analysis with pandas, SQL, charts, and report structure.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby backticks and weak cryptography are false positives caused by markdown code fences and words such as describe or SQL DESCRIBE. The skill is publishable, but it requests Bash plus local file-reading tools, so users should treat it as a medium-risk data workflow skill.

medium_findings.title: Bash and Local File Tool Access
medium_findings.description: The skill permits Bash with Read, Grep, and Glob. This is legitimate for local data analysis, but it can execute commands and inspect local files if the user approves unsafe workflows.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools line confirms Bash and file-reading tools. I found no malicious commands, so the concern is capability risk rather than confirmed abuse.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Findings Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings occur at markdown code fences around Python, SQL, and report examples. They are documentation examples, not executable Ruby backtick calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines are markdown fences or surrounding example boundaries. The surrounding context shows static analysis instructions and sample output, not active shell execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are ordinary data-analysis wording or SQL DESCRIBE usage. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or encryption workflow is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain description, describe, DESCRIBE, and descriptive-statistics text. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or other weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: External Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to official Pandas, Matplotlib, and Seaborn documentation. These are public reference links and do not show data exfiltration or runtime network calls.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs point to official library documentation and appear only in the References section. No code path sends data to these domains.

content.user_title: Analyze Data and Create Insight Reports
content.value_statement: Raw datasets are hard to interpret without a repeatable analysis workflow. This skill guides exploration, cleaning, statistics, visualization, and reporting with pandas, SQL, and charts.
actual_capabilities: Loads and profiles CSV-style datasets with pandas examples.
Suggests common cleaning steps for missing values, duplicates, types, and outliers.
Shows SQL patterns for schema inspection, sampling, and aggregate statistics.
Guides descriptive statistics, grouped analysis, correlation checks, and pivot tables.
Provides visualization patterns for histograms, boxplots, heatmaps, and time series.
Structures a final report with findings, statistics, and recommendations.
limitations: It provides workflow guidance, not a packaged analysis application.
It depends on user-provided data and available local Python or SQL tools.
It does not validate statistical assumptions automatically.
It warns against exposing sensitive personal data but does not enforce redaction.

use_cases.title: Explore a New Dataset
use_cases.description: Profile columns, missing values, data types, ranges, and sample rows before deeper analysis.

use_cases.title: Prepare Business Metrics
use_cases.description: Clean records, group by segments, calculate trends, and summarize results for decisions.

use_cases.title: Create Visual Reports
use_cases.description: Turn distributions, correlations, category comparisons, and time trends into chart-backed findings.

prompt_templates.title: Profile a Dataset
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this dataset structure. Summarize columns, data types, missing values, duplicates, and the first questions I should ask.

prompt_templates.title: Clean Analysis Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this dataset for quality issues. Recommend cleaning steps for missing values, duplicate rows, type conversions, and likely outliers.

prompt_templates.title: Find Segment Insights
prompt_templates.prompt: Group this dataset by the main segment fields. Identify performance differences, trends, anomalies, and metrics worth visualizing.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Executive Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a concise analysis report with dataset overview, key findings, statistical summary, chart recommendations, caveats, and decision-focused recommendations.

output_examples.input: A sales CSV with order date, region, product category, revenue, and customer identifier.
output_examples.output: The result highlights revenue by region, monthly trend changes, high-performing categories, missing customer identifiers, and recommended charts.

output_examples.input: A product usage table with events, users, timestamps, plans, and feature names.
output_examples.output: The result summarizes active users, feature adoption by plan, retention signals, suspicious gaps, and follow-up questions for instrumentation.

output_examples.input: A survey dataset with ratings, demographics, free-text comments, and completion dates.
output_examples.output: The result reports response counts, rating distributions, demographic comparisons, data quality concerns, and cautious interpretation notes.

best_practices: Work on a copy of raw data and preserve the original source.
Start with simple profiling before applying advanced statistics.
Document assumptions, filters, transformations, and limitations in the final report.
anti_patterns: Do not expose sensitive personal data in prompts, outputs, or generated charts.
Do not remove outliers without explaining the rule and business impact.
Do not present correlations or trends as causal claims without supporting evidence.

faq.question: What data formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: It provides guidance for CSV, JSON-style data, SQL tables, and pandas DataFrames.

faq.question: Can it create charts?
faq.answer: Yes. It gives chart patterns for histograms, boxplots, heatmaps, and time series visualizations.

faq.question: Does it run the analysis automatically?
faq.answer: No. It guides the assistant through analysis steps using available tools and user-approved execution.

faq.question: Is it suitable for sensitive data?
faq.answer: Use caution. Remove or mask personal, financial, medical, or confidential fields before analysis.

faq.question: Can it help with SQL analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes SQL patterns for schema review, sampling, counts, distinct values, and aggregate metrics.

faq.question: What should I provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide the dataset, column meanings, business context, analysis goal, and any known data quality concerns.
